stats.fact: GPT-5-Codex was released on September 23, 2025, priced at $1.25 per 1M input tokens and $10.00 per 1M output tokens
GPT-5-Codex supports up to 400K token context window, enabling large-scale codebase operations in a single interaction
OpenAI Codex reached 4 million weekly active developers by April 2026, up from 2 million in early March 2026
Codex usage increased 10× since August 2025; usage in ChatGPT Business/Enterprise grew 6× since January 2026
GPT-5.3-Codex achieves 56.8% on SWE-Bench Pro, 77.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.0, and 64.7% on OSWorld-Verified
GPT-5.5 achieves 58.6% on SWE-Bench Pro and 82.7% on Terminal-Bench 2.0 (agentic/terminal leader)
Claude Opus 4.7 leads SWE-bench Pro at 64.3% versus GPT-5.5's 58.6%, while GPT-5.5 leads Terminal-Bench 2.0 at 82.7%
More than 85% of OpenAI employees use Codex weekly across all departments including finance and marketing
GPT-5.5 uses approximately 40% fewer output tokens to complete the same Codex task compared to GPT-5.4
GPT-5.2-Codex pricing: $1.75 per 1M input tokens, $14.00 per 1M output tokens, with cached input at $0.175 per 1M tokens

angles: Clarify what 'GPT-5-Codex' means vs. the Codex platform: the original gpt-5-codex model (Sept 2025) vs. Codex running on GPT-5.5 — many developers are confused by the naming
Responses API vs Chat Completions: GPT-5-Codex is Responses API-only, a fundamental paradigm shift developers must understand before integrating
When to choose GPT-5-Codex variants vs. GPT-5.5 general model for coding tasks — cost vs. performance tradeoffs for different workloads
Agentic loop design patterns: how to structure long-horizon software engineering tasks (feature dev, refactoring, debugging) with GPT-5-Codex
Token economics and cost optimization: 400K context window, context compaction strategies, cached input pricing
SWE-Bench Pro performance trajectory across the Codex model family — what the benchmarks mean for real production use
AGENTS.md and configuration patterns: how to give GPT-5-Codex durable project-level guidance
GPT-5-Codex in enterprise workflows: how to integrate with MCP, GitHub, and existing CI/CD pipelines
